original_question: ( True and not not not True ) is

So, if we take "not not not True," that would be flipping the switch three times. Starting from True (on), flipping once: False (off), flipping again: True (on), flipping a third time: False (off). So "not not not True" is False in this context. Therefore, the statement "True and not not not True" becomes "True and False," which is indeed False. So the answer seems to correctly model the logical concept.
